best_edges.evidence: 31 trades, $2415.3 PnL, 252.47 PnL/exposure hour, 12.0 minute median hold, 100% win rate. It is the largest contributor and has better efficiency than most multi-trade profiles, despite worst MAE -403.2.
34 trades, $1237.7 PnL, 5.3 minute median hold, average MAE only -74.3, but contains the run worst MAE -527.0. The edge is real, but its tail looks like a distinct extended-VWAP failure mode.

best_edges.edge: breakout_long_1dte fallback when only 1DTE data is valid
best_edges.evidence: 20 trades, $1305.0 PnL, no slow trades, 14.1 minute median hold. It contributes 18.3% of run PnL and validates that 1DTE routing is productive, though average MAE -123.9 is not clean.
5 trades, $985.6 PnL, 234.36 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, median TP 12.0. Sample is small but reward profile is materially different from pullback scalps.
1 trade, $91.0 PnL, 455.0 PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE only -11.7, setup quality 72.5 and ADX 42.8. Too small to optimize aggressively, but worth preserving as a discovery seed.

broken_edges.evidence: Largest profile by trade count among tail-heavy rows; worst MAE -527.0 came from charm_pin_pullback_long after an 80.3 minute hold, poor early path behavior, ES-VWAP distance 26.2, RSI 42.4, ADX 17.95, and negative VWAP acceleration.
broken_edges.problem: Tail-heavy positive-gamma pin pullback entries are masking a failure state inside an otherwise useful fast edge.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE total was only $162.8 across 5 trades. breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades had 52.6 minute median hold, -210.6 average MAE, and only 40.5 PnL/exposure hour.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE stale fallback has poor capital efficiency and elevated underwater behavior.

broken_edges.evidence: Profiles are active in the router list but disabled or produced zero trades in this run: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, trend_day_pullback_long. With only one completed baseline run, enabling all would create router noise.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or dormant active-router profiles lack evidence and should not be tuned opportunistically in the next risk-adjusted pass.

broken_edges.evidence: squeeze_long has 10 trades and $721.0 PnL, but median TP is very large at 33.59 while realized average PnL is 72.1 and average MAE is -103.7. It is profitable, but the TP regime may be relying on partial/order mechanics rather than clean target capture.
broken_edges.problem: Potential mismatch between configured upside target and realized path quality.

market_regime_read: The current leader is a high-hit-rate long-only regime built around valid 0DTE option state, pullback continuation, and positive charm/gamma support. The run is not loss-driven; all completed trades were profitable, so risk-adjusted improvement should come from reducing hidden underwater capital and slow-to-profit tails without cutting the fast winner base. VALID_0DTE is the main engine with 81 trades and $5450.6 PnL, while VALID_1DTE_ONLY is a legitimate secondary regime with 20 trades and $1305.0. STALE_0DTE is profitable but weak and should be treated as a defensive fallback state. The most important new-regime observation is that extended-above-VWAP entries are not uniformly bad: clean extended-VWAP winners exist, so research should split extended continuation from extended rejection rather than bluntly blocking VWAP extension.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate extended-VWAP continuation from extended-VWAP rejection inside charm/pullback entries.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: A broad VWAP max-distance cap would block clean extended-VWAP winners; the bad state is extended plus slow/failed immediate follow-through, weak early path, or rejection near prior high.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Extended VWAP Rejection Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Entry-time ES VWAP distance/slope/acceleration, rolling-high distance and minutes-since-high, first 1-5 minute path response if available for labeling, profile and option data state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Long entry is materially above VWAP and near/rejecting a recent high while momentum confirmation is mixed or deteriorating.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce worst MAE and 60+ minute holds while preserving at least 90% of extended_vwap_winners PnL and keeping total PnL at least 98% of run_001.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dedicated clean 1DTE pullback regime may complement breakout_long_1dte when 0DTE is unavailable.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: The current 1DTE router only uses breakout_long_1dte; it may miss lower-volatility pullback continuation opportunities or route them through an overly breakout-biased profile.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant pullback_long_1dte Activation Test
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades and blocked candidate rows with pullback conditions, 1DTE option map quality, ES structure and VWAP state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Option data state is VALID_1DTE_ONLY and pullback structure is present without full breakout confirmation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Adds net PnL or improves PnL/exposure hour versus breakout_long_1dte alone, with average MAE no worse than breakout_long_1dte and no degradation in VALID_0DTE results.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Mean-reversion/pin-avoid dormant concepts may capture avoided late-pin or wall-pin states without expanding standard breakout risk.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Current enabled long profiles are mostly continuation/pullback. Pin avoidance may be a separate high-quality entry class, but activating it blindly risks overlap and duplicate entries.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Concept Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Blocked rows for late_day_pin_avoid_long or charm_wall_pin_avoid_long, near-wall distance, gamma/charm pin state, subsequent MFE/MAE distribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE state near a pin/wall where current continuation profiles are blocked or disabled.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5-10 trades in replay with positive PnL/exposure hour, worst MAE below current portfolio worst, and low overlap with pullback_long and charm_pin_pullback_long.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use router/profile split logic conceptually: preserve current VALID_0DTE pullback and charm engines, but test a stricter companion condition for extended/rejection charm_pin_pullback_long rows rather than a global filter.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether the charm_pin_pullback_long tail is a separable sub-regime.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm_pin tail occurs when a pin pullback entry is far above VWAP with weak early confirmation or rejection-like structure; a narrower charm-pin variant can preserve fast winners while excluding the 80+ minute tail behavior.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Extended-Rejection Split
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE and average MAE for charm_pin_pullback_long, with charm_pin PnL retention and median hold time as guardrails.
next_experiments.risk: The worst tail still exited profitably, and representative fast winners include some similar rejection signals, so over-tightening can reduce throughput without improving objective score.

next_experiments.config_bias: Compare current 1DTE-only routing against a controlled activation of the dormant pullback_long_1dte concept; keep VALID_0DTE profile behavior unchanged.
next_experiments.goal: Test whether 1DTE-only data supports a second profile split beyond breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY is productive but average MAE is elevated; a pullback-specific 1DTE profile may capture cleaner continuation entries and reduce reliance on breakout-style confirmation.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Router Expansion
next_experiments.primary_metric: VALID_1DTE_ONLY PnL/exposure hour and average MAE versus run_001 breakout_long_1dte baseline.
next_experiments.risk: Only 20 baseline 1DTE trades are available, so added router breadth may overfit and dilute a proven simple fallback.

next_experiments.config_bias: Constrain stale/no-0DTE fallback research to stricter quality and shorter-hold behavior; do not chase PnL expansion in stale state yet.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE trades are profitable but capital-inefficient; stricter fallback selection can improve risk-adjusted score without affecting the VALID_0DTE engine.
next_experiments.name: Stale 0DTE Defensive Fallback Refinement
next_experiments.primary_metric: STALE_0DTE PnL/exposure hour and average MAE, with total PnL retention above 98%.
next_experiments.risk: Sample size is only 5 stale trades, so a filter may look good by removing noise while hurting future fallback coverage.

next_experiments.config_bias: Treat charm_wall_breakaway_long as an incubator: explore slight breadth or confirmation variants only if they preserve its high-MFE character.
next_experiments.goal: Find whether the high-MFE charm wall breakaway edge has more tradeable capacity.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The 5-trade charm_wall_breakaway_long sample has strong PnL per exposure and median MFE, suggesting a separate continuation regime that could scale if router overlap remains low.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Breakaway Capacity Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Incremental PnL and PnL/exposure hour with worst MAE not exceeding current charm_wall_breakaway_long worst MAE by more than 10%.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample and high average MAE mean expansion may discover only rare winners while importing larger tails.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not enable every zero-trade active profile. Select one dormant/disabled concept at a time, with router overlap analysis before activation.
next_experiments.goal: Measure whether currently unused active-router profiles are unreachable, redundant, or valid dormant regimes.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some zero-trade profiles are disabled despite being router-listed; one-at-a-time activation can reveal whether they are genuinely dead or masked by current profile priority.
next_experiments.name: Dead Profile Reachability Audit
next_experiments.primary_metric: New unique trades, overlap rate with current profiles, PnL/exposure hour, and worst MAE by activated profile.
next_experiments.risk: Activating disabled profiles can create duplicate or lower-quality entries that obscure the leader’s clean baseline.

plan_label: new-regime discovery plan from run_001 leader
research_notes: The optimizer should avoid returning broad pruning changes from hidden signatures such as strategy_profile != breakout_long_1dte or es_structure_rising_vwap != False because they retain only about 79-82% of PnL and still leave worst MAE at -527. The better research path is regime splitting: isolate extended/rejection charm-pin tails, test a second 1DTE profile, and harden stale fallback. Current win rate is 100%, so realized-loss filters are not informative; prioritize MAE, time-to-profit, exposure efficiency, and whether TP is reached before MAE. Protect fast_winners and extended_vwap_winners fingerprints when designing any entry-quality gate.
risk_controls: Require every experiment to report total PnL retention versus run_001, worst MAE, average MAE, exposure hours, and PnL/exposure hour.
Reject experiments that improve worst MAE only by deleting more than 20% of total PnL unless they materially improve robust risk-adjusted score.
Keep VALID_0DTE and VALID_1DTE_ONLY attribution separate; do not let stale fallback improvements hide damage to the main engine.
Use one dormant activation at a time with full router replacement semantics and overlap checks.
Do not optimize inactive profiles absent from the active router lists; only consider named dormant profiles from the activation context as controlled activation candidates.
